# Source Card: Risk Factors for Postnatal Growth Faltering and Undernutrition at Discharge in Very Preterm Infants (ESPGHAN Consensus Definitions)

## Summary
This peer-reviewed retrospective study of 416 very preterm / very-low-birth-weight infants operationalizes the recent ESPGHAN consensus definitions for postnatal growth impairment, giving the pediatric CDS module concrete, quotable threshold rules: growth faltering (GF) as a WFA z-score fall ≥ 1.0 SD from birth, and undernutrition (UN) as a WFA or LFA z-score < −2 SD at discharge. It also supplies the at-birth classification cutoffs (SGA < 3rd centile; a composite GR definition) and the ESPGHAN "attention" trigger of z-score drops exceeding 2 SD. All z-scores are computed on INTERGROWTH-21st standards, so every threshold is reference-chart dependent.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) GF = WFA z-score fall ≥ 1.0 SD from birth to discharge — locator: Section 2 'At discharge' — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:e707d6601c2ea9fc57737515518c821473a8ae803f45d638c0bc17983c1ccc6f]"
- (ev_002) UN = WFA or LFA z-score < −2 SD at discharge — locator: Section 2 'At discharge' — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:325ab40922a481aa6519f98c53690d3cd1f06b2b7501a5c8fe51322bc87d87ad]"
- (ev_003) ESPGHAN attention trigger = weight/length z-score drops > 2 SD — locator: Section 1, para 1 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:ea2f1df4dcf95a8580dfe443a5ed205259ddd5f548051430d1498f81dc1ce36c]"
- (ev_004) SGA = birth-weight centile < 3rd — locator: Section 2 'At birth' — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:03d4a98f14d812a8e36d3403eccdcb2f8da39d5fa55afda9fc21b48d3c7389fc]"
- (ev_005) GR = BW <3rd centile or <−2 SD, or ≥3 of five listed criteria — locator: Section 2 'At birth' — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:1d78d5acc960c61044ceb962494478114dfbe20c19158548c07201c5e9f4daf1]"
- (ev_006) Cohort prevalence GF 45.3% / UN 33.1% (n=402) — locator: Section 3 opening — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:f51fbfc4087507acfc74aab19b6d702b9ff3cb4c01fd97aeaeb1b2b541dd2a85]"
- (ev_007) All thresholds computed on INTERGROWTH-21st standards — locator: Section 4 Discussion — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:72a177d819ca909bdecaee052d7dfa14755e6caba6f59cb02807f6c795616bcd]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: very preterm / VLBW infants, GA < 32 weeks and/or BW < 1500 g, both sexes; birth classifications (SGA, GR) assessed at birth, GF/UN assessed at hospital discharge. Single level-IV NICU (Bologna, Italy), infants born 2013-2019.
- Assay/method dependence: all z-scores and centiles are computed on INTERGROWTH-21st prescriptive standards. The thresholds are NOT method-neutral — using a different growth reference (Fenton, older cross-sectional charts) would change z-scores and shift the effective cutoffs. Any CDS rule must carry the chart identity alongside the numeric threshold.
- Lifecycle: thresholds are applied ESPGHAN consensus definitions as cited in this Jan-2026 paper; the upstream ESPGHAN position paper is the canonical version authority. No retirement date stated.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- The ESPGHAN "attention" trigger (weight or length z-score drop > 2 SD, ev_003) functions as a surveillance/escalation flag: such infants "require tailored nutritional support." GF (≥1.0 SD drop) and UN (< −2 SD) similarly identify high-risk phenotypes for intensified nutritional monitoring. These are risk-stratification flags in the source's own framing — the CDS converter must treat them as PROPOSED surveillance triggers, not diagnostic/treatment directives.

## Limitations & conflicts
- These definitions are quoted as APPLIED ESPGHAN criteria; the primary ESPGHAN position paper (cited but not fetched here) is the upstream authority — a separate source card should capture the primary definitions to confirm exact wording/version.
- Prevalence figures (ev_006: 45.3% GF, 33.1% UN) are single-cohort, single-center (Bologna 2013-2019) and are epidemiologic context, NOT reference thresholds — do not generalize as population norms.
- Exact numeric denominators for subgroup percentages (e.g., 89.7%, 85.5%) are cohort-specific and not decision cutoffs; not asserted as CDS thresholds here.
- Chart dependence is a live conflict axis: any other source using Fenton or a cross-sectional reference will report different z-scores for the same infant — preserve as an explicit chart-identity conflict, never average across charts.
- No sex-specific or GA-band-specific numeric threshold is given in the source beyond the population inclusion criteria (GA < 32 wk and/or BW < 1500 g); sex modifies RISK (female protective for GF) but not the z-score cutoffs themselves.
